mission.title: open-webui: enh/issue: performance optimisation
mission.description: enh/issue: performance optimisation. Requested by 14+ users.

steps.title: Check current open-webui deployment
steps.description:
Verify your open-webui version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
helm list -n open-webui 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working open-webui installation.

steps.title: Review open-webui configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant open-webui configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get configmap -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-webui
```
The performance of long chat threads is really bad. I don't know if it is a browser client side issue or an issue on the server side.

steps.title: Apply the fix for enh/issue: performance optimisation
steps.description:
>  This seems to not be an issue with Chromium -- I was able to continue that chat with fast responses for a couple of days, and then... a different issue with long chats on Chromium:
> 
> ```js
> Uncaught (in promise) RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded
>     at Ze (Chat.svelte:604:30)
>     at Ze (Chat.svelte:611:15)
>     at Ze (Chat.svelte:611:15)
>     at Ze (Chat.svelte:611:15)
>     at Ze (Chat.svelte:611:15)
>     at Ze (Chat.svelte:611:15)
>     at Ze (Chat.svelte:611:15)
>   

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get events -n open-webui --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "enh/issue: performance optimisation" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: >  This seems to not be an issue with Chromium -- I was able to continue that chat with fast responses for a couple of days, and then...

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with open-webui installed or the issue environment reproducible.
